title: Rodgers-Tao: de Bruijn-Newman constant Lambda >= 0 (2020)
approach: Proves Lambda >= 0; RH equivalent to Lambda = 0

checks.name: Theorem Lambda >= 0 proven
checks.detail: Published and peer-reviewed in Forum of Mathematics, Pi 8. Widely accepted by mathematical community.

checks.name: No RH proof claimed
checks.detail: Paper explicitly proves only Lambda >= 0. Combined with Polymath15 (Lambda <= 0.22), the gap 0 <= Lambda <= 0.22 remains. RH requires Lambda = 0.

title: Griffin-Ono-Rolen-Zagier: Jensen polynomial hyperbolicity (2019)
approach: Jensen polynomial hyperbolicity for each fixed degree d, large n

checks.name: Jensen hyperbolicity for fixed d proven
checks.detail: Published in PNAS. Widely accepted. For each fixed d, J^d_n is hyperbolic for all sufficiently large n.

checks.name: No RH proof claimed
checks.detail: Paper does not claim to prove RH. Jensen hyperbolicity for each fixed d is partial progress, not a complete proof.

title: Connes spectral program (2026) — detailed audit
approach: CvS spectral operators converge to zeta zeros; det_reg gap is open

checks.name: CvS eigenvalue convergence to zeta zeros
checks.detail: Numerically verified at 6 cutoffs c=13..47 in this repository. Eigenvalue plateau [-80.7, -79.3] OOM for c=23..47 confirmed.

checks.name: det_reg convergence to Xi proven
checks.detail: CCM 2025 Section 8 explicitly identifies this as an open gap. Convergence of det_reg to Xi requires additional analytic input.

checks.name: Paper honest about the gap
checks.detail: Connes' paper explicitly does NOT claim to prove RH. The det_reg gap is clearly labelled as open.

checks.name: Our work bypasses this gap
checks.detail: This work uses Xi(t) = integral Phi(u) cos(tu) du directly. No spectral operator convergence is needed — the Fourier cosine representation is an exact identity.

checks.name: perturbation inequality direction
checks.detail: Inequality goes wrong direction: need C*eps < |Q|, not |Q| < C*eps

checks.name: exp(-t^4) counterexample claim
checks.detail: Incorrect: Csordas-Varga 1989 shows exp(-t^4) has ONLY real zeros.

checks.name: g'' coefficient (numerical)
checks.detail: Rel error: 0.00e+00. g'' coefficient should be 81*pi^2*n^4/2. Derivation: (9/2)^2 * 2 = 81/2.

checks.name: perturbation inequality direction
checks.detail: Inequality goes wrong direction: need C*eps < |Q|, not |Q| < C*eps

checks.name: IA coverage range
checks.detail: IA covers [0.0, 0.5]. Gap: [0.5, 1.0] not certified. Our verification uses [0, 1.0].

checks.name: g'' coefficient (numerical)
checks.detail: Rel error: 0.00e+00. g'' coefficient should be 81*pi^2*n^4/2. Derivation: (9/2)^2 * 2 = 81/2.

checks.name: approach type
checks.detail: Spectral approach (not log-concavity).

checks.name: eigenvector stability assumption
checks.detail: Eigenvector mode count changes from 7 to 22 as cutoff c increases from 13 to 47.

checks.name: peer review status
checks.detail: Not peer-reviewed. Blockchain timestamped only.

checks.name: approach type
checks.detail: Even-dominance (not log-concavity).

checks.name: CAP certificates
checks.detail: Reproduced at 6 lambda values.

checks.name: key step: Proposition A6
checks.detail: Under peer review (2+ months).

checks.name: Lean formalization complete (no sorry)
checks.detail: Theorem 'analytic_louiz_kernel' in §4.2 uses 'sorry'. In Lean 4, sorry admits any goal without proof. This is a proof sketch, not machine-verified.

checks.name: R(c) matches 1/ζ(1/c) numerically at c=2
checks.detail: R(2) = 1.000000, 1/ζ(0.5) = -0.684765, |error| = 1.6848. R→1 because μ(1)=1 makes n=1 terms identical in Sμ and S; all n≥2 terms are super-exponentially small. The kernel does not encode ζ(s).

checks.name: Functional equivalence R(1/s)=1/ζ(s) rigorously established
checks.detail: Paper asserts R(c)→1 as c→∞ and 1/ζ(s)→1 as s→0 are the same function via analytic continuation. This is invalid: analytic continuation from a single boundary limit is not a valid identification of two distinct analytic functions.

checks.name: Internal consistency: §6.1 numerics match Lean definition
checks.detail: §6.1 reports n=1,i=0 term ≈ 6.180e-04 (consistent with exp(-n^c·e^c)). Lean definition uses exp(exp(c)) ≈ exp(1618) at c=2, giving term ≈ 10^{-702}. These differ by ~700 orders of magnitude. The paper's formal definition contradicts its own numerical section.

checks.name: No false ancillary mathematical claims
checks.detail: §5.2 claims Louiz 'disproved the Twin Primes Conjecture via Brun's upper bound'. Brun's theorem (1919) bounds the sum of twin prime reciprocals — it does not disprove TPC. TPC is an open problem.

checks.name: Independently peer-reviewed or expert-verified
checks.detail: Both the primary paper and the validation report are authored by Akram Louiz. Validation was performed by Gemini DeepResearch (AI), not by independent mathematicians. No peer review.

title: Morato de Dalmases: 600-cell Dirac operator (Mar 2026)
approach: Dirac operator on 600-cell Hilbert space => zeta zeros as eigenvalues

checks.name: Scope of claims is realistic
checks.detail: Paper claims to prove simultaneously: RH, GRH, Goldbach Conjecture, Twin Primes Conjecture, and Collatz Conjecture. Each is a separate multi-decade open problem. Claiming all five via one 600-cell geometric framework is a strong red flag.

checks.name: No circular free parameters
checks.detail: 600-cell angular defect delta_0 ~ 6.8 degrees used as confining potential. This parameter is selected to produce the right spectral properties rather than derived from number-theoretic principles.

checks.name: Goldbach derivation logically independent
checks.detail: Goldbach claimed from 'positivity of heat kernel trace'. Heat kernel positivity is automatic for a positive self-adjoint operator and does not directly imply every even integer is a sum of two primes.

checks.name: Peer review status
checks.detail: Not peer-reviewed. 11 companion PDFs not consolidated.

title: Yamaguchi: Gram Jacobi spectral determinant (May 2026)
approach: Gram Jacobi J_N eigenvalues approximate zeta zeros; D_N/xi -> c via Hadamard rigidity

checks.name: Heat kernel trace ratio (skipped in quick mode)
checks.detail: Claimed ratio 0.9999996 at N=100. Computable; consistent with RH.

checks.name: J_inf self-adjointness rigorously established
checks.detail: Self-adjointness of J_inf in N->inf limit requires domain analysis. The Gram Jacobi matrix converges numerically but the functional-analytic closure (dense domain, symmetric operator, deficiency indices) is not rigorously established in the paper.

checks.name: Hadamard rigidity (D_N/xi -> c) rigorously closed
checks.detail: The convergence D_N(z)/xi(1/2+iz) -> c requires uniform convergence of the Hadamard product, which needs eigenvalue spacing estimates. Numerically supported at 10,000 zeros but analytic proof gap remains.

checks.name: Computational methodology quality
checks.detail: 77 C proof programs, 3 independent verification paths (A/B/C), adversarial 'contradiction machine' (80/80 falsified), Bitcoin timestamp. Most rigorous computational methodology among independent attempts.

checks.name: Peer review status
checks.detail: Not peer-reviewed (May 2026 preprint).

title: Singh Khalsa: Li-kernel reduction (Feb 2026, '1% Proof')
approach: Reduces RH to single quantitative inequality on Li coefficients

checks.name: Claims scope is honest (does not overclaim)
checks.detail: Paper explicitly states: 'we do not claim a proof of RH'. The '1%' framing is honest: a reduction, not a completion.

checks.name: Li criterion framework mathematically correct
checks.detail: Li's criterion (lambda_n > 0 for all n iff RH) is established (Li 1997). The Laguerre-weighted representation of Li coefficients is a known approach.

checks.name: Reduction fully closes the proof
checks.detail: The reduction identifies one quantitative inequality that would imply RH, but does not prove that inequality. The inequality itself remains open.

checks.name: g'' coefficient
checks.detail: g'' coefficient should be 81*pi^2*n^4/2. Derivation: (9/2)^2 * 2 = 81/2.

checks.name: perturbation inequality direction
checks.detail: Correctly requires C*eps << |Q_Phi|

checks.name: perturbation constant C magnitude
checks.detail: Claimed C=204. Our verified C=204. Ratio: 0.00e+00

checks.name: IA coverage range
checks.detail: IA covers [0.0, 1.0].

checks.name: IA subinterval count
checks.detail: Claims 52898 subintervals on [0.0, 1.0]

checks.name: counterexample understanding
checks.detail: No incorrect claims
